common.metaDescription: A driver and control utility for portable fan controller devices.

advancedDevices.title: Supported devices
advancedDevices.subtitle: Manage WiFi/virtual serial compatibility devices and user BLE/HID native profiles. BLE/HID profiles are auto-traversed by native connect and are not manually enabled here.

gate.title: Advanced device area
gate.description: This page is for supported-device records, DIY controllers, and raw protocol testing. Wrong commands or mismatched interfaces may cause device errors.
gate.warningTitle: Proceed only if you understand the controller details
gate.warningBody: Confirm the transport, speed unit, endpoints, UUIDs, frame format, checksum, and parser before sending commands or saving a device.
gate.dialogDescription: The next screen can import device definitions and send raw debug commands. Keep ordinary users on the normal Status and Settings pages.

sections.supportedHint: Built-in templates are read-only baselines for packets, speed mapping, parsers, and transport defaults.
sections.userHint: Saved user devices live here. WiFi and virtual serial devices can be enabled manually; BLE/HID profiles stay as native-connect candidates and are traversed automatically.
sections.emptySupported: No supported-device library entries were returned.
sections.debugHint: Use bounded raw-command tests while building a reusable device.

connectionBanner.title: Compatibility device bindings
connectionBanner.description: Only WiFi and virtual serial compatibility devices are bound here. Native BLE/HID devices do not need enabling; scan connects them automatically.
connectionBanner.addHint: Add or import a compatibility device first

hints.basicInfo: These fields define the saved device identity, connection type, speed unit, and speed range used by normal Settings.
hints.deviceFunctions: Only enable functions that this exact device and protocol have verified. Settings and backend calls are both gated by these flags.
hints.wifiConnection: Save the device IP and HTTP endpoints here. Normal Settings may update only the IP property during manual entry or future discovery.
hints.bleConnection: Use scan/probe to fill addresses and UUIDs, but keep final protocol fields editable for DIY controllers.
hints.serialConnection: Port, baud rate, delimiter, and serial framing live here; Settings only chooses the saved device.
hints.protocol: Defines how FanControl sends speed commands and parses state responses for this device.
hints.serialPorts: Pick a detected COM port or type one manually if your controller uses a virtual port.
hints.bleScan: Scan is manual and time-bounded. FanControl uses advertised name and service UUIDs when available; write/notify characteristics can still be entered manually.
hints.bleAddress: Optional. Filled from scan results when available, and used for direct BLE connection or GATT probing.
hints.featureCommand: When this function is enabled, fill the matching command template. Variable syntax follows the packet template.
hints.customCommands: Add verified advanced control commands. Command type automatically forms the device function whitelist; this version only exposes power-on start and smart start/stop.
hints.customCommandTemplate: Variable syntax follows the packet template above. Empty commands are not saved.

placeholders.name: My cooler
placeholders.featureCommand: For example 5A A5 ... or a JSON template
placeholders.customCommandTemplate: For example 5A A5 ... or a JSON template

customCommands.empty: No advanced control commands yet. Add a command to place the matching function in the device whitelist.

dialog.addDescription: Create a device from a template, or enter known controller details manually.
dialog.editDescription: Update the saved device name, connection details, commands, parsers, and speed settings.
dialog.manageDescription: Select or delete devices created by import or manual entry. Built-in templates stay protected.
dialog.deleteDescription: This only removes the saved user device from FanControl. Built-in templates are not changed.
dialog.deleteWarning: If this device is enabled, FanControl will switch to another device for the same connection type when possible.

validation.importRequired: Paste a device import code first.
validation.serialPortsEmpty: No COM ports detected. You can still type the port manually.
validation.bleScanEmpty: No BLE devices were found. You can still enter the matching fields manually.
validation.gattProbeFailed: Could not probe BLE GATT services: {{error}}
validation.gattProbeEmpty: No GATT services were returned. You can still enter UUIDs manually.

importExport.description: Device files and import codes include names, transport settings, speed units, endpoints/interfaces, command templates, parsers, and capabilities.
importExport.exportPlaceholder: Click Generate to create a string starting with FCDP1.
importExport.importPlaceholder: Paste a string starting with FCDP1.
importExport.dropHint: Use a .fcdp, JSON, or text file. Import merges by device ID and keeps existing devices.

profileTest.title: Device test
profileTest.hint: Test the current draft through a temporary backend executor. The device is not saved or enabled.

debug.warning: Raw commands are rate-limited by the backend path, but they still go directly to the device. Use only known-safe commands.
debug.notConnected: Connect a device first to send raw commands.
debug.confirmDescription: This command will be sent through the active device path. Confirm the payload, target device, and wait time before continuing.
debug.saveProfileHint: Use the last successful raw command as a starting point for a reusable device.
debug.profileNotes: Created from a raw debug command. Command: {{command}}. Wait after send: {{waitMs}} ms.

toast.nativeAutoManaged: Native BLE/HID devices are auto-traversed when connecting and do not need enabling in Advanced Devices.

bridgeWarning.default: CPU/GPU temperature reading failed. Try reinitializing temperature monitoring. If the CPU temperature is still empty, install or update PawnIO, or close other hardware monitoring tools.

coreService.unavailable: Core service is unavailable. Check whether FanControl Core was quarantined by security software, or restore/reinstall Core and try again.

history.title: Temperature and fan history
history.disabled: Background recording is disabled. Showing temporary history from this session only.
history.singleSampleCore: 1 background sample recorded. Waiting for more data.
history.singleSampleSession: 1 session sample recorded. Waiting for more data.

mode.smartDescription: Adjusts fan speed automatically based on real-time temperature
mode.manualDescription: You can adjust the mode and parameters in Settings

maxRpmHint.bs1: This device supports 0-100% fan speed control.
maxRpmHint.max4000: The full speed range is unlocked. Maximum fan speed is 100%.
maxRpmHint.max3300: The available speed ceiling is limited by device power or configuration.
maxRpmHint.max2760: The available speed ceiling is limited by device power or configuration.
maxRpmHint.unknownCode: The device reported an unmapped maximum gear code: {{code}}
maxRpmHint.waiting: Waiting for the device to report speed control capability.

hero.coreUnavailable: Core service is unavailable. Restore the background service first.

disconnected.title: Device not connected
disconnected.description: Connect a device to search for available hardware.
disconnected.descriptionWithDevice: {{name}} is the enabled device. Check its connection type and address, then connect.

bridgeWarning.default: The temperature bridge failed to read data. Try reinitializing temperature monitoring and try again.
bridgeWarning.sharedProcess: This instance is reusing a shared process

aboutPanel.title: About {{name}}
aboutPanel.description: {{name}} supports multiple cooler and fan-controller profiles across WiFi, BLE, virtual COM/serial, and HID/RPM paths, reading state and sending speed commands according to each device capability.

version.noPrereleaseFound: No pre-release build is available right now.
version.checkFailed: Failed to check updates. Please try again later.
version.emptyReleaseNotes: No release notes available, or this fetch failed.
version.upToDate: You already have the latest version
version.noInstallerHint: This release has no auto-installable package. Please download it from the release page.
version.downloadingHint: The installer will start automatically when the download completes
version.installingHint: FanControl will restart automatically when installation finishes
version.doneHint: The installer has started; FanControl will close shortly
version.updaterWindowBody: Installing the new version. Please do not close this window

contact.title: Project links
contact.tagline: New releases, issue feedback, and device adaptation progress are tracked here.

items.question: Why does the CPU temperature show 0°C?
items.answer: This usually means the temperature bridge has not received sensor data yet. Start the app as administrator, confirm the bridge component is not blocked by security software, then restart the app or the computer if the value remains 0°C.

items.question: Which devices are currently supported?
items.answer: The built-in default device is Slim pressure fan cooler Pro over WiFi HTTP. FanControl also provides device profiles and templates for WiFi, BLE, virtual COM/serial, and HID/RPM controllers; DIY devices need correct connection, packet, and parser settings for their MCU protocol.

items.question: Can existing settings continue to work?
items.answer: The portable directory settings.json is read first, and common settings from earlier FanControl builds remain compatible, including device IP, control mode, temperature source, manual speed, and fan curve.

manualGear.title: Manual gear
manualGear.editHint: The 12 gears must increase from low to high, max {{max}}
manualGear.unavailable: The device is disconnected or does not support manual gears
manualGear.runtimeUnavailable: {{gear}} gear is not unlocked with the current power input. {{limit}}

learning.title: Adaptive learning
learning.description: After longer runtime, the target % at each temperature point is fine-tuned automatically so real temperatures stay closer to the configured curve. Learned offsets can be reset at any time.
learning.targetDescription: Adaptive learning tries to settle steady-state temperature near this ceiling. Lower values favor cooling; higher values favor silence.
learning.offsetDescription: The main % corrections of the current learned curve relative to the base curve.

balanced.label: Balanced
balanced.description: Allows learned adjustments above and below the base curve.

cooling.label: Cooling first
cooling.description: Only allows learned % increases, avoiding higher idle temperatures.

quiet.label: Quiet first
quiet.description: Only allows learned % decreases, avoiding unnecessary fan ramp-up.

prediction.title: Thermal anticipation
prediction.description: Uses power draw and rising temperature trends to make small early fan adjustments before heat catches up.

hints.dragPoint: Drag the blue points to adjust %

history.detailsDescription: The curve page keeps a 1-hour history overview so you can compare the current curve against recent temperature trends.
history.emptyEnabled: Background recording is enabled. Waiting for more temperature and fan samples.
history.emptyDisabled: Background recording is currently disabled. You can enable it on this page.
history.waitingMoreSamples: 1 sample recorded. Waiting for more data before drawing the trend chart.
history.displaySettingsDescription: Choose which trend lines to show and adjust the hover tooltip order.

profiles.title: Curve profiles
profiles.createDescription: Create a new profile from the current curve without changing the original profile.
profiles.manageDescription: Rename the current profile, or import, export, and delete profiles.
profiles.unsavedSwitchDescription: Save the current changes or discard them before switching profiles.
profiles.deleteDescription: Delete “{{name}}”? The adjacent profile will become active.
profiles.deleteUnsavedDescription: “{{name}}” has unsaved changes. Deleting it will discard those changes too.

importExport.title: Import / export curve profiles
importExport.description: Use a profile code or .fcurve file
importExport.exportHint: Copy a profile code for quick sharing or save a file for backup. Unsaved curve changes are saved first.
importExport.importPlaceholder: Paste a profile code starting with B2C1.
importExport.dropHint: You can also drop a .fcurve or text file here

warning.title: Notice
warning.body: Very low speed may reduce cooling performance. Adjust according to device status.

toast.learningResetDescription: The fan curve will now follow your configured values exactly

light.smartTempWarning: In Smart Temp mode the device controls lighting automatically, so color and brightness cannot be adjusted manually.
light.applyHintDisconnected: Will apply automatically on next connection

fan.autoControlDescription: Adjust fan speed automatically based on the temperature curve
fan.autoControlApplyFailed: Failed to switch automatic temperature control: {{error}}
fan.sampleSmoothingDescription: EMA weighting: larger values are smoother and steadier, smaller values respond faster. Keep the default if unsure.
fan.temperatureBaselineDescription: Choose the thermal source, GPU device, and specific sensor used for fan control. On multi-GPU laptops you can lock the dGPU here.
fan.gpuLowPowerProtectionDescription: Auto mode checks whether the dGPU is active before reading GPU temperature and power. Switch to Always read when forced GPU telemetry is needed.
fan.processorDeviceHintDetected: The detected processor updates automatically with TempBridge.
fan.processorDeviceHintWaiting: CPU model not detected yet. It will appear automatically once TempBridge is available.
fan.noCpuTemperatureData: No CPU temperature data is currently available
fan.gpuDeviceHintAuto: Auto mode will prefer the discrete GPU when possible.
fan.gpuDeviceHintNotPolled: The dGPU is inactive and was skipped; turn off low-power protection to force reading.
fan.gpuDeviceHintLocked: Locked to the GPU device you selected.
fan.gpuTemperatureNotPolled: The dGPU is inactive, so GPU temperature was not read
fan.noGpuTemperatureData: No GPU temperature data is currently available
fan.currentControlSource: Fan control is currently using the {{source}} baseline at {{temperature}}°C.
fan.noControlTemperature: No valid control baseline temperature is available yet.
fan.transientSpikeFilterDescription: Ignore isolated temperature spikes so one-off jumps do not disturb smart control sampling and response.
fan.learningDescription: After longer runtime, the target % at each temperature point is fine-tuned automatically so real temperatures stay closer to the configured curve. Learned offsets can be reset at any time.

prediction.title: Thermal anticipation
prediction.description: Uses power draw and rising temperature trends to make small early fan adjustments before heat catches up.

fan.temperatureHistoryDescription: When enabled, the core service keeps the last hour of CPU/GPU temperature curves. When disabled, temporary curves are drawn only while the GUI is open.
fan.curveProfileDescription: Switch fan control curves directly from settings without changing pages.
fan.manualGearDescription: Choose a gear and speed level. Applying it switches back to manual mode and sends the command to the current device.
fan.manualGearDisconnected: Connect a device before sending a manual gear.
fan.manualGearUnavailable: The device is disconnected or does not support manual gears
fan.customSpeedDescription: Fixed speed for the current device in special scenarios
fan.customSpeedWarning: Custom speed disables smart temperature control
fan.customSpeedRangeHint: Manual speed range is {{min}} to {{max}}. Adjust carefully for the cooling load.

groups.basicDescription: Runtime toggles exposed by the current device capability whitelist.
groups.smartStartStopDescription: Shows firmware-backed start/stop behavior according to the current device capabilities.
groups.lightingDescription: Shown only when the active device explicitly supports lighting.

device.gearLightDescription: Control the gear indicator light on the device
device.powerOnStartDescription: Automatically run when the device receives power
device.screenDescription: This device marks screen capability. Runtime screen controls will be opened after a verified whitelist is available.
device.smartStartStopDescription: Choose when cooling stops after the system shuts down
device.wifiSmartStartStopDescription: Let the new firmware handle start/stop from the PC heartbeat
device.wifiSmartStartStopTip: Requires firmware that supports the FanControl WiFi v2 protocol.
device.wifiSmartStartStopStandbySpeedDescriptio: The firmware uses this speed after the PC heartbeat times out. 1% is recommended.

legionFnQ.enableDescription: Listen for Legion performance mode changes, including Fn+Q and Lenovo software switches.
legionFnQ.takeOverDescription: When a performance mode change is detected, switch the cooler to the mapped manual gear below.
legionFnQ.mappingDescription: The speed level on the right is a low/medium/high sub-level within the same fan gear and affects actual speed percentage.

toasts.saveFailed: Failed to save Fn+Q plugin settings: {{error}}

system.themeDescription: Follow the system by default, lock to light/dark, or pick a custom theme from the install themes folder.
system.windowBlurDescription: Choose Acrylic, Mica, Mica Alt, or Off. Built-in light and dark themes adapt automatically; custom themes are unchanged.
system.windowBlurTip: Restart the app for this setting to take effect.
system.languageDescription: Change the app language instantly and remember the choice automatically.

hotkeys.title: Global hotkeys
hotkeys.description: Click a field on the right and press a key combination. It saves automatically on blur. Press Backspace/Delete or use the clear button to leave it empty and disable the hotkey.

manual.title: Cycle manual gear
manual.description: Cycle through Quiet, Standard, Strong, and Overclock while preserving each gear's sub-level memory.

auto.title: Toggle smart control
auto.description: Quickly switch smart temperature control on or off for game and quiet scenarios.

curve.title: Switch temperature curve
curve.description: Quickly rotate curve profiles for work, gaming, or night-time scenarios.

toasts.duplicate: The three hotkeys cannot use the same combination

system.autoStartTip: Running as administrator avoids a UAC prompt each time
system.reconnectDescription: Continue using the app configuration after reconnecting
system.reconnectTip: Recommended to prevent fallback to the device default mode after disconnects

deviceConnection.title: Device connection
deviceConnection.description: BLE/HID are scanned automatically; enable WiFi/COM in compatibility mode.
deviceConnection.autoScanDescription: Scans matching rules from imported or manually configured BLE/HID devices. Configure and enable WiFi and virtual COM below.
deviceConnection.bluetoothScanOnlyHint: Automatically traverses HID profiles first, then BLE profiles. Use compatibility mode below for WiFi and virtual serial.
deviceConnection.discoveredDevicesDescription: Shows connectable devices found in this scan.
deviceConnection.discoveredDevicesEmpty: Scan finished. No identifiable BLE/HID device was found.
deviceConnection.pairedDevicesDescription: Comes from Advanced Device Settings; Settings only selects the saved device to use.
deviceConnection.pairedDevicesEmpty: No saved device yet. Add or import one in Advanced Device Settings.
deviceConnection.wifiDiscoveredEmpty: WiFi discovery will be added later; enter the IP manually below for now.
deviceConnection.wifiManualAddHint: WiFi only: update the IP of the currently saved device. Future discovery will write to this same property.
deviceConnection.autoPathHint: BLE/HID devices are matched from the device library rules.
deviceConnection.autoScanEmptyState: No supported auto-identifiable BLE/HID device was found. Make sure the device is powered on and connected to this PC; use compatibility mode below for WiFi and virtual COM.
deviceConnection.compatibilityDescription: For DIY controllers, older devices, or devices that cannot be identified through BLE/HID. Turning this off does not delete profiles.
deviceConnection.wifiCompatibilityDescription: For DIY controllers, older devices, or WiFi devices that cannot be identified automatically. Enable it to bind a device and maintain its IP connection property.
deviceConnection.serialCompatibilityDescription: For DIY controllers, older devices, or serial devices that cannot be identified automatically. Enable it to bind a device and maintain its COM/baud connection properties.
deviceConnection.wifiHint: This saves the IP connection property on the bound device and syncs the legacy IP field for compatibility. Advanced packets, protocols, and parsers are managed in Devices. Future discovery or reconnect logic should only update this IP property.
deviceConnection.bleHint: BLE scan can help complete or verify matching details for native BLE profiles. UUID and protocol details stay in Devices.
deviceConnection.bleScanEmpty: No BLE devices were found. Keep the current device or add details in Devices.
deviceConnection.serialHint: Maintain the virtual serial port, baud rate, and protocol in Advanced Device Settings; Settings only selects the saved device to enable.
deviceConnection.hidHint: HID profiles are traversed automatically by Connect Device and do not need compatibility-mode enabling.

toasts.profileRequired: Add or import a device for this connection type in Devices first.
toasts.autoScanMultiple: Multiple matched devices found. Choose one to connect.
toasts.autoScanComplete: Scan complete. Choose a device to connect.
toasts.autoScanFound: Found {{count}} identifiable device(s). Choose one from the scan results.
toasts.wifiScanEmpty: No available WiFi device found yet
toasts.bleSaveFailed: Failed to save BLE device details: {{error}}
toasts.serialPortRequired: Enter a COM port or virtual port name.
toasts.scanPartialFailed: Scan finished, but part of the scan failed: {{error}}
toasts.connectRejected: The device did not report a successful connection

deviceConnection.serialPortsEmpty: No COM ports detected. You can still type a virtual port manually.
deviceConnection.connectedDevicesDescription: Current device state for this connection path.
deviceConnection.wifiScanDescription: Find an available IP through the current saved WiFi device state endpoint. Normal search checks the current address, same subnet, active adapters, and Windows hotspot first; deep scan checks common subnets first, then expands the search range if needed.
deviceConnection.wifiScanResultsEmpty: No device was found yet. Make sure it is powered on and on the same network, then try deep scan; expanded deep scan may be slower.
deviceConnection.wifiScanRunning: Looking for WiFi devices. Current saved address and common LAN ranges are checked first.
deviceConnection.wifiDynamicIPDescription: Use this for Windows hotspot or other cases where only the last IP number may change. If your router reserves a static IP, you usually do not need it.
deviceConnection.wifiDynamicIPTip: When connection fails, FanControl only changes the last IP number in the saved subnet and updates this device's IP property if found.
deviceConnection.wifiReconnectDescription: Later versions can scan the same subnet after disconnects and update the saved device IP.
deviceConnection.wifiReconnectPending: Reserved for the future WiFi scanning implementation. It does not scan in the background yet.
deviceConnection.serialInterfaceDescription: Serial interface details are maintained in Advanced Device Settings; Settings only reads the selected device summary.
deviceConnection.serialInterfaceManagedInDevices: Edit port, baud rate, packet protocol, and parser rules in Advanced Device Settings.
deviceConnection.wifiScanRunningExpanded: Expanding the search range; this may be slower

offline.message: Device is not connected. Some features are unavailable.

debug.dangerousCommandWarning: High-risk command 0x{{command}}: this directly touches low-level firmware or debug registers. Misuse may make the device behave abnormally or become unusable. Confirm before sending.
debug.rawCommandWarning: Raw commands are sent directly to device firmware. Wrong commands may cause abnormal device behavior, so proceed carefully.
debug.pawnDescription: Temperature reading looks wrong? Try reinstalling the PawnIO driver.

toasts.uninstallWarning: PawnIO uninstall step returned a notice: {{warning}}

bullets.disableAutoControl: • Smart temperature control will be disabled
bullets.fixedSpeed: The fan will run at a fixed speed

alerts.lightStripFailed: Failed to apply light strip settings: {{error}}
alerts.deviceCommandFailed: Failed to send the device command. Check logs or export diagnostics.
alerts.deviceCommandFailedWithError: Failed to send the device command: {{error}}

immediate.label: Immediate
immediate.description: Respond immediately to system load changes

delayed.label: Delayed
delayed.description: Delay response to avoid frequent start/stop cycles

smart_temp.label: Smart temperature
smart_temp.description: Switch lighting automatically based on temperature
